Former Japanese school principal Yuhei Takashima, aged 64, admitted to paying for sex with over 12,000 women, some as young as 14, during repeated visits to the Philippines over a quarter-century. His case, under investigation since 2013, involves allegations of child pornography and production of obscene acts, for which he was arrested by Japanese police in January 2014. Police officers seized 147,600 photos from Takashima's possession. Takeshi Akimoto, the police officer in charge of crime involving minors in Kanagawa, stated that the incident occurred during his tenure as a junior high school principal at a Japanese school in Manila from 2008 until his retirement in 2011. Yokohama education authorities expressed regret over the allegations, if proven true.

A Brazilian online football store is selling next season's alleged Chelsea kit with and without Gareth Bale's name on the back, featuring the incoming 'Yokohama' sponsor but missing the Adidas emblem. Reports link Bale to a return to the Premier League due to his struggles at Real Madrid in Spain. Meanwhile, Drogba shirts are also available despite his uncertain future at Chelsea.
